Original Requirements:
6. Community Social Networking Platform

A social media platform where users build profiles, share posts, and engage with friends and communities, while admins keep the platform organized through basic moderation tools.

Core features:

- Let users register via email/password with email verification before login is allowed
- Allow users to build a profile with a profile picture, cover photo, bio, and location
- Let users create text posts with optional single or multi-image upload, and edit/delete their own posts
- Let users like a post (toggle like/unlike) and see a simple like count
- Let users comment on posts (flat, non-nested) and edit/delete their own comments
- Show a basic news feed listing posts from friends/followed users sorted by most recent first (simple chronological query)
- Let users send and accept/reject friend requests, with a friends list page
- Let users follow/unfollow other users without requiring mutual approval, with a followers/following count
- Let users create Groups with a name, description, and public/private flag, and join/leave public groups directly
- Let group creators approve or reject join requests for private groups
- Show a dedicated group feed listing only posts made within that group
- Provide a basic direct messaging feature: 1:1 text conversations stored and retrieved like a simple chat log (no real-time typing indicators or read receipts)
- Build a notifications list (likes, comments, friend requests) generated on action and marked read/unread on click
- Implement basic keyword search across users, posts, and groups using simple text matching (LIKE/contains query, no full-text engine required)
- Let users bookmark/save posts into a single default 'Saved Posts' list (no custom collections)
- Provide a 'Report Post' button that creates a report record with a reason dropdown, viewable in an admin list
- Build a simple admin panel where admins can view reported content and delete a post or suspend a user account (boolean active/suspended flag)
- Let users set basic privacy on their profile (public or private) controlling whether non-friends can view their posts
- Let users block another user, which hides each other's posts and prevents messaging
- Let users update notification preferences with on/off toggles per category (likes, comments, friend requests)
- Let admins manually grant or revoke a 'verified' badge on a user account (boolean flag toggle)
- Let users view their own post history and edit account settings (email, password, username)
- Implement basic pagination/infinite scroll on feeds, comments, and search results (limit/offset queries)
- Let users delete their own account, which soft-deletes their data (sets a deleted flag rather than hard deletion)
- Enforce two roles only — Admin and User — with simple route guards (no granular permission matrix), and log admin actions (ban/delete/verify) to a basic activity log table
Product Goals: Deliver a reliable and intuitive social networking experience for end-users
Provide effective moderation and administration tools to maintain a healthy community
Ensure scalability and performance with basic pagination and efficient queries
User Stories: As a new user, I want to register with my email and verify it so I can start using the platform securely.
As an active user, I want to create and share posts with images, and engage with friends through likes and comments.
As a community builder, I want to create both public and private groups, and manage membership requests for private ones.
As a privacy-conscious user, I want to set my profile to private and block users who bother me.
As an admin, I want to review reported posts, suspend problematic accounts, and grant verified badges to trusted users.
Competitive Analysis: Facebook: Rich feature set, strong moderation, but complex privacy controls.
Twitter: Simple feed, real-time engagement, but limited group functionality.
Reddit: Strong community/group focus, nested comments, but overwhelming for casual users.
Mastodon: Decentralized, user-controlled, but lacks polished admin tools.
LinkedIn: Professional networking, verified profiles, but not suitable for casual socializing.
Nextdoor: Hyper-local communities, moderation tools, but limited scalability.

Requirement Analysis: The platform requires a comprehensive set of social networking features including user registration with email verification, profile management, post creation with images, like/comment interactions, friend/follow system, groups (public/private), direct messaging, notifications, search, bookmarking, reporting, admin panel with moderation actions, privacy controls, block user, notification preferences, verified badges, account settings, pagination, soft-delete, and role-based access. All features should be implemented without excessive complexity (e.g., flat comments, simple chronology, LIKE queries, no real-time). Security and data integrity are important, with soft-deletion and admin action logging. The architecture should support future scalability via basic pagination. The requirement is clear overall; potential ambiguity lies in the exact data models for blocking (mutual hide) and notification categories, but these can be resolved during design.
Requirement Pool: User registration with email verification and authentication (email/password, login, session management)
Core social features: profile creation, posting (text + images), like/unlike, flat comments, news feed (chronological from friends/follows)
Friend request system (send, accept/reject) and follow/unfollow functionality with counts
Groups: create (public/private), join/leave public groups, approve/reject private group requests, group-specific feed
Admin panel: view reports, delete posts, suspend accounts (active/suspended flag), grant/revoke verified badge, and log admin actions
UI Design draft: A responsive web interface with a clean, modern layout. The top navigation bar includes a search box, notification bell icon, and user avatar dropdown. The main feed displays posts in card format with user info, timestamps, like/comment buttons, and image carousels. Profile pages show cover photo, profile picture, bio, location, and tabs for posts, friends, and groups. Groups have a dedicated page with group info, member list, and feed. The admin panel is a separate dashboard with tables for reports, users, and activity logs. Modals are used for posting, commenting, and editing. Mobile-friendly with hamburger menu.
Anything UNCLEAR: The exact implementation details of 'blocking' (whether it hides both users' content from each other entirely or just prevents interaction) and the scope of 'notification preferences' (whether they affect in-app notifications, email, or both) are not specified but can be clarified during development. Also, the requirement for 'soft-delete' needs to define what data remains accessible to admins.
